oneLiner: This work matters because before 2013 NYC workers had no guaranteed right to paid time off when sick, which is why the City Council passed the Paid Sick Leave Law that year and later created a dedicated worker-protection office to enforce it and other labor standards.
summary: New York City became the seventh, and largest, jurisdiction in the country to guarantee paid sick leave when the City Council overrode a mayoral veto to pass the Earned Sick Time Act in 2013, later expanded in 2014 to cover roughly 500,000 more employees. In 2016, the agency's mandate grew further with the creation of the Office of Labor Policy & Standards, its central resource and advocate for workers. That expanded worker-focused role led the agency to rename itself from the Department of Consumer Affairs to the Department of Consumer and Worker Protection in 2019.

sources.label: NYC's Protected Time Off Law - DCWP
NYC Department of Consumer Affairs Changes Name and Expands Mission
